title: pages data model with unique slug per section
description: Add the `pages` table (`id, section_id, titulo, slug, ordem`) to the Drizzle schema, with a uniqueness constraint on `slug` scoped to its parent section.
acceptanceCriteria: `pages` table exists with `id, section_id (fk -> sections.id), titulo, slug, ordem`
A composite unique constraint exists on `(section_id, slug)`, allowing the same slug to be reused across different sections but not within the same section
Migration applies cleanly on top of prior migrations
Deleting a section cascades to its pages at the database level or is explicitly handled at the application layer (must match the decision made in TASK-18)

steps.details: Add a Drizzle `uniqueIndex` on `(sectionId, slug)` so slugs are unique per section, not globally.
Run `drizzle-kit generate`, review the resulting SQL for the foreign key and unique index, and confirm it matches the cascade decision made in TASK-18.
Insert two pages with the same slug in the same section and confirm the second insert fails; insert two pages with the same slug in different sections and confirm both succeed.

technicalNotes: Slug uniqueness is scoped per-section (not global) because two different sections may reasonably want a page called e.g. 'overview'.
